title: 10 best herbal teas for post nasal drip

teas.tea_desc: Slippery elm tea provides relief from post nasal drip due to its demulcent properties, which mean it has a soothing, protective filming effect. This film, created by the release of mucilage when the tea contacts mucous membranes, reduces irritation. As a result, the overproduction of mucus in the nasal passages slows, alleviating the sensation of post nasal drip. Slippery elm's high mucilage content also helps to lubricate and moisturize inflamed tissues, further minimizing the symptoms of post nasal drip. Additionally, this tea's anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties help fight off possible infections causing excessive mucus production responsible for post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Bark: The bark of the slippery elm plant contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film in the mouth and throat, providing relief from post nasal drip irritation.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and mucous membrane irritation caused by post nasal drip.

Buds: The buds of the slippery elm plant are a source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
Seeds: Slippery elm seeds contain a high concentration of mucilage, making them an effective demulcent, providing a soothing coating to the throat and reducing irritation from post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried slippery elm bark.
Place bark in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the bark.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then remove the bark.
Drink the tea slowly, up to three times a day for post nasal drip relief.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: This gel-like substance can help soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation, providing relief for post-nasal drip.
Tannins: These astringent compounds can help tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing excessive mucus production associated with post-nasal drip.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants may help reduce inflammation, providing relief for swollen nasal passages and facilitating mucus drainage.
Sterols: These natural anti-inflammatory agents can help reduce inflammation and irritation, promoting mucus flow and alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.
Saponins: These naturally occurring compounds have been shown to have antibacterial properties, which may help control bacterial infections contributing to post-nasal drip.
Essential oils: The volatile oils found in slippery elm can have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which may relieve symptoms of post-nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea provides relief for post nasal drip due to its demulcent properties, which imply that it releases a mucilaginous substance that creates a protective film in the mouth and throat. This soothing film, in turn, reduces irritation and inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating discomfort caused by persistent mucus accumulation. Moreover, marshmallow tea's antitussive effects further contribute to post nasal drip relief by suppressing chronic coughing, which often exacerbates the condition. Additionally, the hydrating nature of the tea thins out the mucus, promoting effortless expulsion and reducing its overall production. Lastly, research indicates that certain constituents of the marshmallow plant exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, further aiding in the reduction of post nasal drip symptoms and fostering overall respiratory health.
tea_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow flowers help soothe irritated throat membranes and reduce post nasal drip.
Leaves: The soft, velvety leaves contain mucilage that helps relieve irritation, inflammation, and congestion in the respiratory tract, easing post nasal drip.
Roots: Packed with medicinal mucilage, marshmallow roots have demulcent properties that coat and soothe mucous membranes, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds, like the rest of the plant, contain mucilage that helps alleviate inflammation, making it an effective aid in managing post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 teaspoons of marshmallow root for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey to the marshmallow tea for sweetness.
Squeeze half a lemon into the tea for added vitamin C.
Drink the marshmallow tea warm, up to 3 times a day for post nasal drip relief.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: The mucilaginous constituents of marshmallow tea form a soothing film over mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation caused by post nasal drip.
Ascorbic acid: Acting as an antioxidant, ascorbic acid supports immune function, helping the body to combat the infections that can often accompany post nasal drip.
Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from symptoms associated with post nasal drip.
Phenolic acids: With their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, phenolic acids in marshmallow tea can help alleviate inflammation and fight infections related to post nasal drip.
Tannins: Astringent tannins found in marshmallow tea can help reduce mucus production and soothe inflamed mucous membranes, offering symptomatic relief from post nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea provides relief for post nasal drip due to its active constituent, glycyrrhizin. This compound fights inflammation by inhibiting the production of key inflammatory molecules, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes. Consequently, licorice tea soothes irritated mucous membranes, reducing excessive mucus secretion and alleviating post nasal drip symptoms. Additionally, glycyrrhizin exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract. Lastly, licorice tea's demulcent qualities form a protective film over the mucous membranes, further preventing irritation and minimizing post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and soothes irritated throat membranes, providing relief for post nasal drip.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory ailments, including post nasal drip, by promoting mucus membrane health.
Flowers: Licorice flowers contain isoflavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages caused by post nasal drip.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain coumarins, which have antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate coughing fits related to post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root solids.
Optional: add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for post nasal drip relief.
tea_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This compound in licorice tea can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from post nasal drip.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants present in licorice tea can help soothe irritation and inflammation in the throat, useful for managing post nasal drip.
Coumarins: Certain coumarins found in licorice tea, like umbelliferone, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which may alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils (such as anethole and limonene) in licorice tea have been found to have expectorant and decongestant properties, helping to clear excess mucus in cases of post nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Goldenrod tea provides relief from post nasal drip due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the compound called kaempferol. This compound helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion. Additionally, goldenrod tea contains tannins known for their astringent qualities, which tighten and dry mucous membranes, decreasing the production of excess mucus. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties, attributed to the essential oil content, can help fight off infections causing post nasal drip. By sipping warm goldenrod tea, the steam inhaled aids in clearing nasal passages, providing immediate and soothing relief. Ultimately, goldenrod tea's diverse beneficial compounds make it an ideal natural remedy for post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Roots: The goldenrod's roots contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation, providing relief for post nasal drip.
Leaves: The leaves of goldenrod are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, known to soothe irritated throats and alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.
Flowers: Goldenrod flowers have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for treating post nasal drip caused by infections.
Stems: The stems of goldenrod contain essential oils with calming and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve post nasal drip.
Buds: Goldenrod buds are a powerhouse of medicinal compounds that reduce mucus production, providing relief from post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: Chop 1-2 tablespoons of fresh or dried goldenrod flowers and leaves.
In a tea infuser or mesh strainer, place the chopped goldenrod.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the infuser or directly into the cup.
Steep the goldenrod for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain the tea.
Drink the tea up to three times a day for post nasal drip relief.
tea_constituents: Phenolic acids: They have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and throat, alleviating post-nasal drip.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants have been shown to have anti-allergic properties, which can help reduce symptoms of post-nasal drip caused by allergies.
Saponins: They have expectorant properties that can help clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from post-nasal drip.
Volatile oils: They have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help fight infections causing post-nasal drip.
Essential fatty acids: They have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and throat, alleviating post-nasal drip.
Tannins: They have astringent properties that can help tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing post-nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Yerba santa tea provides relief from post-nasal drip due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal tea, derived from the leaves of the yerba santa plant, contains abundant flavonoids and essential oils. These compounds work together to stimulate the discharge of mucus, easing congestion in the respiratory tract. Moreover, yerba santa tea soothes inflamed membranes, reducing irritation in the throat caused by persistent post-nasal drip. As a result, this tea brings swift, effective relief from bothersome post-nasal drip symptoms.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in volatile oils and rosmarinic acid, leaves are ideal for making medicinal tea to alleviate post nasal drip.
Stems: Containing tannins and flavonoids, stems provide anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for post nasal drip relief.
Flowers: With high amounts of antioxidants, flowers contribute to soothing effects on respiratory issues like post nasal drip.
Seeds: Seeds are a natural source of mucilage, promoting a demulcent action in teas for post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: Measure: Grab 2 tablespoons of dried Yerba Santa leaves.
Steep: Steep the leaves in boiling water (8oz) for 10-15 minutes.
Strain: Strain the tea to remove the leaves, leaving only the liquid.
Sweeten (optional): Add honey or lemon to taste.
Sip: Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.
tea_constituents: Eucalyptol: This compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and thinning out mucus, making it easier to expel.
Limonene: This compound acts as a natural decongestant and has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can contribute to post nasal drip.
Cineole: Also known as eucalyptol, cineole has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to thin out mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Alpha-pinene: This compound has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to thin out mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Beta-pinene: This compound has expectorant and antibacterial properties, helping to thin out mucus and fight off bacterial infections that can contribute to post nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Echinacea tea assists with post nasal drip due to its proven anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal tea, derived from the echinacea plant, reduces inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating irritation caused by excessive mucus. Its immune-boosting abilities shorten the duration of colds and flu, common causes of post nasal drip. Furthermore, echinacea's expectorant qualities help clear airways of mucus, providing relief from related symptoms. Finally, echinacea tea's antimicrobial and antiviral effects inhibit further infection, promoting overall respiratory health and reducing post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of echinacea contain active compounds that support the immune system, making them effective in alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Aerial Parts: Aerial parts of echinacea, such as leaves and flowers, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritation in the throat and nasal passages.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, echinacea flowers help reduce inflammation and strengthen the immune response, providing relief for post nasal drip sufferers.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves contain tannins that have astringent properties, helping to tighten and dry mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip.
Seeds: Echinacea seeds are a source of essential fatty acids and phytosterols, providing anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting benefits, which aid in managing post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers or 4 echinacea tea bags.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and strain the tea into a teapot or a cup.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
tea_constituents: Cichoric Acid: This polyphenol has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping reduce post-nasal drip by alleviating inflammation in the nasal passages.
Essential Oils: The volatile oils, such as echinacea oil, exhibit antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in reducing post-nasal drip caused by infections or irritation.
Polysaccharides: These compounds found in echinacea, like Arabinogalactan, stimulate the immune system, assisting in combating the underlying causes of post-nasal drip.
Alkylamides: These lipophilic compounds have immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory properties, helping alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages and reducing post-nasal drip.
Vitamin C: This essential vitamin present in echinacea supports the immune system, helping to address the root cause of post-nasal drip and promoting overall well-being.

teas.tea_desc: Fenugreek tea alleviates post-nasal drip due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the nasal passages and throat. This herbal tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective coating on the mucous membranes, soothing irritation. Furthermore, fenugreek's natural expectorant qualities help eliminate excess mucus, while its soothing warmth provides comfort and relief. Additionally, fenugreek exhibits antibacterial properties, combating potential infections contributing to post-nasal drip.
tea_parts: Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for post nasal drip due to their mucus-reducing properties.
Leaves: Fenugreek leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful in tea to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Flowers: Fenugreek flowers contain bioactive compounds that help soothe irritated throats and reduce post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: In a small pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of dried fenugreek seeds, reduce heat, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the seeds.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat, for immediate relief.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: Fenugreek tea contains a type of fiber known as mucilage, which can help soothe irritated tissues in the throat and reduce the symptoms of post nasal drip by providing a protective barrier.
Galactomannan: This polysaccharide found in fenugreek has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, reducing post nasal drip.
Saponins: Fenugreek tea contains saponins, which have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the respiratory tract and alleviate post nasal drip.
Diosgenin: This compound in fenugreek has been found to have antihistamine effects, which can help reduce the production of mucus in the nasal passages and provide relief from post nasal drip.
Trigonelline: Fenugreek contains this alkaloid, which has been shown to have antibacterial properties. This can help eliminate infections that may be causing or exacerbating post nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Plantain tea provides relief from post nasal drip due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plantain herb, from which the tea is derived, contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film on the mucous membrane, reducing irritation. This film also helps to lubricate and moisturize the throat, limiting excessive mucus production. Furthermore, plantain tea's demulcent properties alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract, diminishing the severity of post nasal drip symptoms. Finally, the antibacterial agents present in plantain tea inhibit the growth of infection-causing microorganisms, addressing one of the potential causes of post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in mucilage, the leaves help soothe irritated throats and reduce post nasal drip.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of post nasal drip.
Seeds: High in medicinal compounds, seeds can help decrease inflammation and mucus production, easing post nasal drip.
Bark: Has expectorant and demulcent properties, helping to expel mucus and providing a soothing effect on the throat.
tea_recipe: Peel and slice 2 ripe plantains, then add them to a pot of water (4 cups).
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove plantain pieces, then let it cool for a few minutes.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste, stirring until fully dissolved.
Serve the plantain tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed.
tea_constituents: Mucilage: It forms a soothing film over the mucous membranes, relieving irritation and reducing inflammation, thus alleviating post-nasal drip.
Tannins: They have astringent properties, helping to tighten and dry up excess mucus secretions, providing relief from post-nasal drip.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the respiratory tract, thereby easing post-nasal drip symptoms.
Chlorogenic acid: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat infections and inflammation that can cause post-nasal drip.
Gallic acid: With its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, gallic acid helps reduce inflammation and fight infections associated with post-nasal drip.
Caffeic acid: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in soothing inflamed tissues and providing relief from post-nasal drip symptoms.

teas.tea_desc: Thyme tea brings relief to post nasal drip due to its potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Thyme, the herbal ingredient in the tea, contains compounds called flavonoids that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thus alleviating excess mucus production. Moreover, thyme's volatile oils, specifically thymol, work as a powerful expectorant, helping to loosen and expel mucus from the throat and airways. As a natural antimicrobial agent, thyme also combats infection-induced post nasal drip by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses. By sipping warm thyme tea, both the soothing heat and herbal benefits bring immediate comfort and promote healing in the irritated tissues of the throat. Therefore, thyme tea's multifaceted actions make it an ideal natural remedy for post nasal drip.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, thyme leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for post nasal drip due to their potent antimicrobial and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids, thyme flowers produce a soothing medicinal tea that helps reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by post nasal drip.
Stems: Although less potent than leaves or flowers, thyme stems still contain therapeutic compounds, making them suitable for medicinal teas aimed at alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Seeds: Thyme seeds, while often used for seasoning, can also be used in medicinal teas. They possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to manage post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves.
Sip the warm thyme tea, allowing it to soothe your post nasal drip.
tea_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antiseptic and antibacterial properties which can help reduce inflammation and fight infection in the respiratory tract, alleviating post nasal drip.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antispasmodic properties that can help relieve cough and spasm in the throat, reducing post nasal drip.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, reducing post nasal drip.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce post nasal drip.
Volatile oils: The volatile oils in thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, have expectorant properties that can help clear mucus and reduce post nasal drip.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea brings relief to post nasal drip due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal tea, made from Matricaria recutita flowers, contains flavonoids that reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. Its soothing warmth also helps to alleviate congestion by stimulating the cilia, tiny hair-like structures, to move mucus out of the nose and throat. Furthermore, chamomile's antimicrobial and antiviral attributes aid in fighting infections causing excess mucus production. Lastly, the calming effects of chamomile tea on the nervous system can indirectly help post nasal drip by reducing stress-induced inflammation and mucus build-up.
tea_parts: Flowers: The chamomile flowers contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from post nasal drip.
Leaves: The leaves of chamomile contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate irritation and inflammation caused by post nasal drip.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in essential oils, which have calming and soothing effects that can help reduce irritation and discomfort caused by post nasal drip.
tea_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot, reaching a temperature of 205-212°F.
Place one tea bag or 1-2 teaspoons of loose chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or empty teapot.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile in the teapot or teacup, allowing it to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea bag or infuser from the teapot or teacup to prevent over-steeping.
Enjoy the warm chamomile tea, adding honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
tea_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and throat, alleviating post nasal drip.
Chamazulene: With its strong anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, chamazulene can help soothe irritated mucous membranes and relax muscles, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and calming properties, which can help alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages and throat, as well as fight any potential infections contributing to post nasal drip.

Matricin: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help reduce inflammation and mucus production in the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip symptoms.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has antihistamine, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation, allergic reactions, and oxidative stress that may contribute to post nasal drip.

other_remedies: Post nasal drip, characterized by an excess production of mucus in the back of the throat, often responds well to natural remedies. One such remedy is a saline nasal spray, which helps to thin and drain the mucus, reducing congestion. Gargling with warm salt water several times a day is another effective method, as it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the throat. Furthermore, increasing hydration by drinking plenty of water, clear broths, and decaffeinated teas can also help to thin the mucus and promote drainage. Lastly, using a humidifier in the home or office adds moisture to the air, further helping to alleviate the symptoms of post nasal drip.
causes: Allergies: Allergies trigger the release of histamine, a chemical that increases mucus production and leads to post nasal drip.
Cold or Sinus Infection: Infections cause inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, leading to excess mucus production that drips down the back of the throat.
Deviant Septum: A deviated septum can cause uneven airflow and increased mucus production, resulting in post nasal drip.
Dry Air: Dry air, often caused by air conditioning, heating, or dry climates, dries out the mucous membranes, leading to increased mucus production and post nasal drip.
Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease: Stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus can trigger the release of chemicals that stimulate mucus production, resulting in post nasal drip.
Hormonal Changes: Hormonal fluctuations during pregnancy, menstruation, or menopause can affect the nasal membranes, leading to increased mucus production and post nasal drip.
Nasal Polyps: Growths in the nasal passages, known as nasal polyps, can obstruct airflow and increase mucus production, causing post nasal drip.
Pollutants and Irritants: Exposure to pollutants, tobacco smoke, and strong odors can irritate the nasal membranes, stimulating mucus production and post nasal drip.
Sinusitis: Inflammation of the sinuses can lead to excess mucus production, which drips down the back of the throat, causing post nasal drip.
intro: When it comes to alleviating postnasal drip, certain herbal teas have proven to be highly effective. Peppermint tea is a top choice, as its cooling properties help to thin out excess mucus and reduce congestion. Ginger tea is another excellent option, as its natural anti-inflammatory properties work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thereby minimizing drips and discomfort. Echinacea tea is also a popular choice, as its immune-boosting properties help to combat underlying infections that can contribute to postnasal drip. By incorporating these herbal teas into your daily routine, you may find relief from the constant sniffles and congestion.
